title: nasa humboldtiana subsp. glandulifera
intro: Nasa humboldtiana subsp. glandulifera, also known as Andean mint or glandular nasa, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the Andean region of South America and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and wounds, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Nasa humboldtiana subsp. glandulifera can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
